assertions.text: V1 was traveling westbound in lane 2 of a five-lane undivided roadway (two westbound lanes, two eastbound lanes, center turn lane).
V1 traversed the lane lines and entered lane 1 before departing the left side of the roadway.
V1 departed the left side of the roadway and initiated a clockwise yaw.
V1's left side impacted an overhead traffic signal pole.
Driver ejection occurred (occupant ejected from the vehicle) after the left-side impact.
After left-side impact with the pole, the vehicle rotated rapidly clockwise around the pole as it split into two pieces.
The vehicle split into two distinct pieces (front and rear) as a result of the impact/rotation.
The rear portion (rear box) came to rest in the center of the roadway.
The front portion continued westerly after separating and impacted a concrete electric box with its right-side plane.
After impacting the concrete electric box, the front portion came to rest facing easterly.
The impact with the overhead traffic signal pole and subsequent events were severe enough to split the vehicle into two pieces and eject the driver.

verdicts.evidence: The narrative statement about roadway layout and lane number is not represented in the vehicle telemetry; the available data shows vehicle pre-crash speed values but contains no lane or heading/GPS bearing to confirm westbound or lane 2. For example, speed samples show [first value 156.2](T2) km/h and [last value 122.4](T2) km/h but no heading or lane information in the window info (see channels list in [T1]).

Lane traversal (moving from lane 2 to lane 1) and departure on the left are not encoded in the telemetry channels provided. Sensors include speed [first 156.2](T2) km/h and steering peak [peak_raw_value -98.7](T2) deg at [t=-0.9](T2) which show large steering input but cannot confirm lane changes or which side was departed without GPS/vision data (channels list in [T1]).

Telemetry shows a large steering excursion with peak signed dev [ -98.7 deg at t=-0.9 s](T2) and an impact delta-V lateral of [4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T1), but axis alignment to vehicle yaw direction is undocumented so a clockwise yaw cannot be confirmed from device-frame channels alone. Therefore direction of yaw is unverifiable.

Telemetry records an impact pulse with cumulative lateral delta-V [4.3 km/h](T1) and longitudinal delta-V [7.7 km/h](T2), indicating a significant impact, but does not identify impacted object or which vehicle side contacted it; therefore 'left side impacted an overhead traffic signal pole' is unverifiable from sensors alone.

EDR summary shows driver belt code [1](T1) (belted) and airbag stage null in summary, and sensors show strong crash pulses (delta-V long [7.7 km/h](T2), delta-V lat [4.3 km/h](T1)), but occupant ejection is not recorded in these telemetry channels and cannot be determined from them.

Telemetry detects an impact pulse (delta-V long [7.7 km/h at t=0.251 s](T2), delta-V lat [4.3 km/h at t=0.21 s](T1)) and large steering rates before impact ([max_rate_of_change_per_s 447.0 at t=-0.5 s](T2)), but the specific sequence 'rotated rapidly clockwise around the pole as it split into two pieces' requires positional/visual data not present here.

The sensors record multiple impact-related detections and a crash pulse (see delta-V long [7.7 km/h](T2)), but there is no direct telemetry field indicating the vehicle split into two pieces; structural separation is not encoded in these channels.

Rest positions of rear and front portions are spatial/location information not present in telemetry; available data shows final crash-pulse values (delta-V long final [7.7 km/h](T2)) but nothing about resting location.

Claim about front portion impacting a concrete electric box on its right-side plane requires scene/location/contact-side detail; telemetry provides impact magnitudes (delta-V lat [4.3 km/h](T1), delta-V long [7.7 km/h](T2)) but not which object or which vehicle side impacted post-separation.

Final orientation 'facing easterly' is a heading/pose not recorded in the provided telemetry; available channels include speed and crash pulses (e.g. delta-V long [7.7 km/h](T2)) but no final heading.

Severity claim about being severe enough to split the vehicle and eject driver: telemetry shows a crash pulse with cumulative longitudinal delta-V final [7.7 km/h](T2) and lateral delta-V peak [4.3 km/h](T1), and the EDR summary reports max longitudinal delta-V [10.85 km/h](T1) (EDR summary value), indicating a measurable crash pulse; while telemetry cannot confirm structural separation or ejection, the statement that events were 'severe' is supported by recorded crash pulse magnitudes (citing [7.7 km/h](T2) and [4.3 km/h](T1) and EDR summary max [10.85 km/h](T1)).

result.rationale: Most narrative assertions involve scene, lane, heading, or occupant ejection details that are not encoded in the vehicle's telemetry. The sensors do record a measurable crash pulse (delta-V longitudinal and lateral) and large steering excursions prior to impact, which supports that a crash occurred and had non-trivial severity, but do not confirm directional, positional, or structural fragmentation details.

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
